toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dumps

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dumps

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run these tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host. 

To collect crash dumps when a test host crashes, I can use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable (or `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump` for older runtimes). This is the .NET runtime's built-in crash dump collection mechanism.

However, the more modern and test-specific approach is to use the `--blame-crash` option with `dotnet test`, which is part of the blame data collector. This will:
1. Collect a crash dump when the test host crashes


2. Store the dump files in the test results directory

I'll run the command with the `--blame-crash` flag to enable this functionality.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dumps

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-b111d5b43caa46afa1aafc400bface82 && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with blame-crash for crash dumps", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command to run tests with crash dump collection is:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a full crash dump (`.dmp` file) in the test results directory if the test host process crashes. No crashes occurred in this run since all tests passed.
